name: ExtensionAdministrableProductDefinition_Device
title: R5: A device that is integral to the medicinal product, in effect being considered as an "ingredient" of the medicinal product (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `AdministrableProductDefinition.device` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`AdministrableProductDefinition.device` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DeviceDefinition)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `AdministrableProductDefinition.device` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DeviceDefinition)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `AdministrableProductDefinition.device` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `AdministrableProductDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `AdministrableProductDefinition.device` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: A device that is integral to the medicinal product, in effect being considered as an "ingredient" of the medicinal product (new)
element.comment:
Element `AdministrableProductDefinition.device` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `AdministrableProductDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `AdministrableProductDefinition.device` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: A device that is integral to the medicinal product, in effect being considered as an "ingredient" of the medicinal product
element.definition: A device that is integral to the medicinal product, in effect being considered as an "ingredient" of the medicinal product. This is not intended for devices that are just co-packaged.
